comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
## Epic Purpose

Refactor Plexus console chat to use Tactus infrastructure for proper context management, fixing the \"do that again\" continuity failures caused by aggressive message truncation.

## Context

Current state:
- DynamoDB query hard-caps at 16 messages
- TAC compresses to 8 turns with 180-char truncation per message
- Tool responses, system messages excluded from history
- No audit trail of exact LLM context sent per invocation

This breaks conversational continuity when users say "do that again" after long exchanges.

## Goals

1. **Observability**: Capture exact LLM request context for every invocation to S3 (14-day retention)
2. **Continuity**: Token-budgeted history management using Tactus primitives
3. **Memory**: Rolling summary/compaction when history exceeds budget
4. **Migration**: Replace TAC-based approach with Tactus DSPyAgentHandle

## Architectural Boundary

**Tactus** (reusable infrastructure):
- Message history management with token budgeting
- Auto-compaction with pluggable CompactionStrategy interface
- Rolling summary generation
- DSPyAgentHandle with audit hook points

**Plexus** (domain-specific):
- DynamoDB ChatMessage persistence
- S3 audit artifact upload and lifecycle
- Privacy filtering by owner user ID
- Console-specific compaction config
- Tool access mode enforcement

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Console chat uses Tactus DSPyAgentHandle instead of TAC
- [ ] Every LLM invocation has S3 audit artifact with exact context
- [ ] Token budgeting replaces hard message caps
- [ ] Rolling summary auto-compaction works in Tactus
- [ ] "Do that again" works after 20+ turn conversations
- [ ] Privacy filtering preserved
- [ ] Turn latency ≤ baseline (within 10%)
- [ ] All behavior specified in Gherkin
- [ ] All specifications green

## Plan Reference

Detailed implementation plan: `~/.claude/plans/there-are-two-projects-quirky-noodle.md`
